task_completion.description: Open the baseline model and select the Wing component
task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenVSP window is open showing the eCRM-001 model, and the 'Wing' component is selected in the Geometry Browser.

task_completion.description: Add a new Subsurface and set its type to Tank
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Sub' (Subsurface) tab is active in the Wing component dialog, a subsurface has been added, and its Type dropdown is set to 'Tank'.

task_completion.description: Configure the U and W spanwise/chordwise boundaries
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Subsurface tab, the U boundaries are set to Start: 0.15, End: 0.70. The W boundaries are set to Start: 0.20, End: 0.65.

task_completion.description: Run an analysis to compute the tank volume
task_completion.visual_evidence: An OpenVSP analysis window (such as CompGeom or Mass Properties) is open and displays the computed theoretical internal volume for the tank.

task_completion.description: Save the modified model as eCRM001_wet_wing.vsp3
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Save As' dialog or the main OpenVSP window title bar shows the file being saved as 'eCRM001_wet_wing.vsp3' in the /home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/ directory.

task_completion.description: Write the formatted fuel capacity report with correct calculations
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or terminal output shows the file '/home/ga/Desktop/fuel_capacity_report.txt' containing exactly the requested headers, 'Tank Volume: [value] m^3', and 'Total Fuel Mass: [value] kg'. The mass value must be the volume multiplied by 804.

integrity.description: Agent used the OpenVSP GUI to modify the model
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show active interaction with the OpenVSP graphical interface (clicking tabs, entering values in the Subsurface dialog) rather than just editing the .vsp3 XML file in a text editor.

integrity.description: Agent extracted the volume from an actual OpenVSP analysis
integrity.visual_evidence: The volume value written in the text report matches the volume shown in the OpenVSP CompGeom/Mass Properties results window, proving it was not hallucinated.

integrity.description: Agent actually created the report file on the Desktop
integrity.visual_evidence: A text editor save dialog, terminal command (like echo or nano), or file manager shows the report being explicitly saved to /home/ga/Desktop/fuel_capacity_report.txt.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Verified parameters: U start = 0.15, U end = 0.70, W start = 0.20, W end = 0.65. Fuel density = 804.0 kg/m^3. The VLM must verify that the Total Fuel Mass in the report is mathematically correct by multiplying the extracted Tank Volume by 804.0.
